summary: High‑energy set with blazing Red Rooster & The Other One > Satisfaction
blurb: The show crackles with a scorching Little Red Rooster solo and an electrifying The Other One → (I Can't Get No) Satisfaction stretch. Jerry’s improvisational drive shines, Brent’s keyboards are in top form, and the first set builds relentless momentum.
review: On May 17 1981 at the Onondaga County War Memorial the Dead delivered a high‑octane performance that still feels fresh. The opener rolls from Alabama Getaway into The Promised Land before diving into a scorching Little Red Rooster where Brent Mydland’s overdriven solo and scatting steal the spotlight. Jerry Garcia, despite a brief lyric slip in Alabama, launches into a mind‑blowing improvisation on The Other One that carries straight into a rousing (I Can't Get No) Satisfaction encore, fire‑spiking the crowd. Throughout the night Brent’s organ work shines—especially on Wharf Rat—and Bob Weir’s vocal ad‑libs add extra excitement. The first set’s momentum never wanes, and the second set stays jam‑filled and tight, making this a must‑listen snapshot of the band’s 1981 firepower.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four out of six recordings are clearly positive, repeatedly praising The Other One → Satisfaction stretch, Brent's Red Rooster solo and organ work, and overall high energy. No consistent negative comments appear, so a strong positive sentiment adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Bright front‑of‑board tape captures the full energy; with a little EQ it balances the treble and delivers the standout Red Rooster solo and The Other One → Satisfaction segment clearly.

key_highlights: Brent's overdriven Little Red Rooster solo and scatting are show‑stopping.
The Other One → (I Can't Get No) Satisfaction stretch ignites the crowd with wild vocal ad‑libs.
First set builds relentless momentum, highlighted by Alabama Getaway into The Promised Land.

band_performance.Jerry: Sharp, improvisational lead especially on The Other One; a brief lyric slip in Alabama Getaway but still jams strongly.
band_performance.Bob: Vocal improv and wacky ad‑libs on The Other One → Satisfaction fire up the audience.
band_performance.Brent: Overdriven Red Rooster solo, scatting, and killer organ work on Wharf Rat; consistently energetic throughout.
